learning_outcomes:
STATN_OUTCOMES_ENG 1. Major Programme: Knowledge
Outcomes Content
Graduates
will have possessed fundamental knowledge of statistics, including the
following: (1) Basic concepts in
probability and their applications (2) Basic concepts in
statistics and their uses (3) Mathematical tools
for statistics (4) Applied
statistical methodologies (5) Applications of
statistical packages in data management and analysis Critical
Thinking/Statistical Reasoning Graduate
will have well-developed sense of statistical consciousness, and ability in
the following areas: (1) Ability to
consider problems from different perspectives (2) Ability to
identify and criticize the misuse of statistics (3) Ability to analyse
quantitative evidence in a critical manner Skills
Outcomes Generic
Competencies Graduates
will have possessed the following generic skills: (1) Effective
presentation skills to communicate findings/conclusions (2) The ability to
work in teams (3) Competence in
using information technology Professional
Skills Graduates
will have acquired a good mastery of professional skills, including the
following: (1) Ability to
formulate statistical problems (2) Ability to analyse
data and use statistics accurately (3) Ability to
communicate and convey the results of the analysis to anyone Attitude/Value
Outcomes Graduates
will have acquired a positive attitude towards statistical issues, including
the following: (1) Awareness of the
importance of statistics in daily life (2) Appreciation of
different aspects of knowledge in the Information Age (3) Avoidance of the
abuse of statistics (4) Appreciation of
statistical theory 2. Minor Programme: Upon
the completion of the Minor Programme, students will have demonstrated basic
understanding of fundamental issues related to Statistics such as sense of
statistical consciousness and competence in information technology, acquired
knowledge of data analysis and statistical inference, and to pursue a related
career.
study_scheme:
Statistics Statistics Applicable
to students admitted in 2024-25
There are three
streams of specialization: the stream of Data Science and Business
Statistics, the stream of Statistical Learning and Data Mining and
the stream of Data Analytics. A student’s selected stream will be
printed on his/her transcript. All students are under the stream of Data Science and Business Statistics unless they apply at their final
year of attendance to specialize in the stream of Statistical Learning
and Data Mining or the stream of Data Analytics and select the
relevant courses as prescribed below.
Major Programme
Requirement Data Science and
Business Statistics Stream
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 63 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
Group
E: STAT1011 (preferred) or 1012
Group
C: MATH1010 or 1018 or 1520
A
course from the following Group
A: LSCI1001 or 1002 or 1012 Group
B: CHEM1070 or 1072 or 1280 Group
D: PHYS1001 or 1002 or 1111 or 1113
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  27
MATH1030 or 1038 or 1550, STAT1013, 2001, 2005, 2006,
2011, 3001, 3008, 3011
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  27
(a)  |  Eight courses from the following (with at least five
from STAT or RMSC courses coded at 4000 or above level): RMSC4004#, STAT3003, 3005, 3006, 3007, 3009, 3010, 3210,
4001, 4002, 4003, 4004, 4005, 4006, 4008, 4010, 4012
(b)  |  One capstone/research
course from STAT4011 or 4013
Total:  |  63
Explanatory
Notes:
1. 2.  |  STAT courses at 2000 and above level as well as those
labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification. Students admitted to the Dual Undergraduate Degree
Programme (DDP) with The University of Edinburgh (UoE) are required to
follow the major programme requirement of the Data Science and Business
Statistics Stream, and will not be able to declare other streams.

Learning and Data Mining Stream

In addition

criteria as specified by the Faculty can take the following stream
offered by the Faculty:
Science,
Technology And Research Stream
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 12 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required
Courses:
(a)  |  One Faculty Package Course:  |  3
Choose from the two remaining groups of the Faculty
Package that have not been used to fulfill the Faculty Package
Requirement
(b)  |  Research Courses:  |  6
STAR2000, 3000, 4000[a]
(c)  |  Seminar Courses:  |  3
STAR2050, 3050, 4050
2.  |  Experiential Learning:  |  ---
At least 4 consecutive weeks of outside Hong Kong
exposure[b]
Total:  |  12
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  STAT courses at 2000 and above level as well as those
labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
[a]  |  Students may select research-oriented course(s), as
approved by the Major Programme, to substitute up to 4 units for
fulfillment of Research Courses requirement.
[b]  |  Students must complete any
exchange/research/internship programme(s) offered by the University,
Colleges, the Faculty of Science or Major Programme, as approved by the
Major Programme, to fulfill the Experiential Learning requirement. Students
are responsible for the extra costs incurred in the exchange/research/internship
programme(s).

Explanatory Notes:
[a]  |  The recommended course pattern of College GE courses
varies slightly depending on students’ College affiliation. Students who
are required to take 4 units of College GE should take two 2-unit courses
at different terms.
[b]  |  Students taking a term and a year load exceeding the
maximum will need to seek approval from the Registrar.
[c]  |  Students are required to take MATH1050 as a substitute
for STAT1013.
[d]  |  Students should take one 2-unit UGEA course and one
2-unit UGEC course.
[e]  |  MATH10095 taken at UoE can be transferred to fulfill
the requirement of STAT4003.
[f]  |  MATH10007 taken at UoE can be transferred to fulfill
the requirement of STAT3007.
[g]  |  MATH10093 taken at UoE can be transferred to fulfill
the requirement of STAT3006.
[h]  |  40 credits taken from MATH10010, 10063, 10102 or 11127
at UoE can be transferred to fulfill the requirements of STAT3011 and 4011.
[i]  |  MATH10064 taken at UoE can be transferred to fulfill
the requirement of STAT4002.
[j]  |  Students are required to complete at least 42 units
(out of 63) of major courses offered by CUHK.
